content:
10 min read
Just now

--

You upgraded to Java 21 or later, your tests still pass — but now the JVM warns that an agent was loaded dynamically. See why Mockito and VisualVM both trip it, what JEP 451 changed, and the right fix for each.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Mockito and VisualVM trigger the same Java 21 dynamic-agent warning through different workflows.You upgrade a project to Java 21, run mvn test, and your green build is suddenly carrying a warning it didn't have before:
WARNING: A Java agent has been loaded dynamically
(...\byte-buddy-agent-1.14.10.jar)WARNING: Dynamic loading of agents will be disallowed by default
in a future release
Nothing failed. Java has not detected malware.
The warning means that Mockito loaded an agent after the test JVM had already started. That agent gives Mockito’s inline mock maker access to Java’s instrumentation API, allowing it to transform loaded classes.
VisualVM produces the same warning for a different reason: when you start CPU or memory profiling, it dynamically loads its profiler agent into the running application.
Both are legitimate uses of a powerful JVM capability. JEP 451 makes that activity visible and prepares for a future JDK where dynamic agent loading must be explicitly approved by the application owner.
